summary: Mechanical clang-format cleanup merged across 36 files, mainly ground/stage sources plus small effect and menu touches. The diff reorders includes, wraps or unwraps long lines, reformats repeated initializer entries, and adjusts comments/prototypes without evidence of semantic logic changes. No PR body, review comments, or issue comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Ran clang-format over 36 files with 48 added and 78 deleted lines.
Reflowed many ground/stage OSReport calls for failed HSD_GObj allocation from split argument lists into one-line calls when they fit, preserving message strings and arguments.
Sorted or regrouped include blocks in several ground files, including moving gr/grzakogenerator.h before gr/inlines.h and placing <dolphin/os/OSError.h> before baselib includes in touched files.
Moved "sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.h" into the quoted include block in src/melee/gr/grpushon.c.
Wrapped a long commented-out efSpecial_SpawnSpecial/va_arg line in efSync_Spawn.
Wrapped the long fn_802190A0 declaration in src/melee/gr/grpushon.h while preserving parameter order and the address comment.
Condensed repeated zero AnimLoopSettings initializers in mn_803ED294 in src/melee/mn/mnruleplus.c.
smart_moves: Kept formatting-only churn isolated in a dedicated PR, making the change quick to inspect and reducing noise for later semantic decompilation work.
Applied the formatter consistently across related stage factory files, normalizing repeated OSReport/Ground_SetupStageCallbacks patterns.
Preserved existing symbols, comments with address labels, string literals, and function call arguments; the diff evidence points to style-only edits.
Used clang-format to settle include-order and line-wrapping questions mechanically instead of relying on one-off manual formatting.
decomp_lessons: Separate clang-format churn from matching or naming PRs so reviewers can distinguish mechanical whitespace/include changes from decompilation logic.
Do not infer semantic meaning from line breaks in OSReport calls, long prototypes, initializer lists, or comments; clang-format may reflow them automatically.
When touching stage files, expect repeated factory helpers around HSD_GObj creation and Ground_SetupStageCallbacks to be formatted consistently.
Header declarations with address comments, such as fn_802190A0 in grpushon.h, can be wrapped by clang-format while retaining the address annotation and signature.
Include ordering is part of the project style; future PRs should run the formatter rather than hand-sorting touched include blocks.
naming_conventions: No new naming convention was introduced by this PR.
Existing prefixes were preserved: efSync_/efAsync_/efSpecial_/efLib_ for effect code, grFigure*/grT*/grPushOn_ for ground/stage code, and mn_803ED* for menu data symbols.
Existing decomp address-style names and comments were retained, including fn_802190A0 and grPushOn_80218ED0-style declarations.
The PR did not normalize parameter names; existing names such as gobj_id, arg0, id, and index remained as they were in their respective files.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching tactic or code-generation strategy was documented in the PR slice.
The useful matching workflow lesson is procedural: land formatter-only changes separately so future matching diffs are not polluted by clang-format output.
Because this is a formatting PR, future reuse should be limited to style baselining and diff hygiene, not inferred compiler-behavior fixes.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, reviews, or PR body text were present in the provided slice.
Merged within minutes, suggesting the repository accepted this as mechanical formatting cleanup; no explicit reviewer rationale is available.
follow_up_queries: git log -- src/melee/gr src/melee/ef src/melee/mn after PR 2311 to find semantic changes that used this formatting baseline
grep for OSReport("%s:%d: couldn t get gobj in src/melee/gr to compare remaining stage factory formatting patterns
review .clang-format include category rules before manually changing <dolphin/os/OSError.h>, baselib, or sysdolphin include order
search future PRs for mixed clang-format churn plus matching changes to identify review-risk patterns
